summary: Worked on the engineering team for the Transnordestina Railway — then the largest railway under active construction in Brazil (~1,700 km). The project connected agricultural production regions in northeastern Brazil to Atlantic export ports, carrying national economic significance. Responsibilities spanned the full civil scope of the infrastructure: earthworks, soil analysis and granular layer design, rail superstructure, surface and subsurface drainage, bridges, viaducts, tunnels, and signaling systems.
highlights: Review and adaptation of executive designs across earthworks, drainage, and rail superstructure scopes
Regulatory compliance review and preparation of as-built documentation for active construction fronts
Quantity surveying and cost tracking across multiple simultaneous construction zones
Soil analysis and granular layer evaluation for subgrade and sub-ballast design
Coordination with multidisciplinary teams across structural, drainage, and signaling scopes

summary: Extensive field and plant experience in asphalt concrete production and flexible pavement construction, including airport runway trial sections. Responsible for laboratory QA/QC of asphalt mixtures, compaction monitoring, mineral skeleton optimization, and surface texture and friction evaluation. Pioneered the local introduction of advanced pavement surface technologies.
highlights: Led mix design and implementation of the first Porous Friction Course (OGFC) sections in Fortaleza — a genuine local first
Led design activities for the first Highly Modified Asphalt Binder pavement sections in the city
QA/QC oversight of asphalt production and field compaction for airport runway pavement trial sections
Laboratory analysis for mineral skeleton optimization (aggregate gradation and compactability)
Surface texture and friction evaluation on completed pavement sections
